name: The End of the Rope-Walk
description: The long, low rope-walk building ends in a yard open to the harbor wind; coils of rope and hemp bales make good cover, and the water is close enough to hear. It is a place people come when they do not want to be found.
atmosphericDetails: The harbor smell is strong here — brine, fish, and the cold iron smell of the water at night.
The rope-walk building blocks the view from the street; what happens here is invisible from Ann Street.
A single lantern on a post at the water's edge swings in the wind, its light unreliable.
